steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The blank describes how a country’s policy environment relates to swap-line decisions. The decisive clue is the probability data: 0.20 for open-capital countries versus 0.03 for restrictive ones — a sharp gap showing policy environment was meaningfully relevant to the outcome.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — material to: Means significant or relevant to. The large gap between 0.20 and 0.03 shows policy environment was genuinely significant to swap-line decisions — a precise fit. ✓
  • Choice B — predicated on: Means based entirely upon or dependent on as a foundation. This overstates the relationship: policy environment influenced outcomes but didn’t solely determine them (the probability was 0.20, not 1.0). ✗
  • Choice C — decoupled from: Means unrelated to or separated from. The stark probability gap proves the opposite — policy and swap lines were clearly linked, not independent. ✗
  • Choice D — mediated by: Means transmitted or controlled through an intermediary. This implies a third factor bridges policy and swap-line decisions, but the passage describes a direct relationship with no intermediary mentioned. ✗
That matches Choice A — material to.
